indexes.name: idx_lineitem_orderkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Supports repeated joins and correlated subqueries on lineitem by order key in Q3, Q4, Q9, Q11, Q17, Q20, Q22, including EXISTS/NOT EXISTS probes and GROUP BY/HAVING on l_orderkey.
indexes.benefits: Reduces cost of joining orders to lineitem and speeds repeated per-order existence checks and aggregations.
indexes.tradeoffs: Adds write and storage overhead on the largest fact table.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_partkey_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Targets joins and correlated lookups by part and supplier in Q8, Q19, and Q20, especially the Q20 subquery filtering lineitem on l_partkey = ps_partkey and l_suppkey = ps_suppkey with date filtering.
indexes.benefits: Improves access into lineitem for part/supplier-driven joins and correlated checks.
indexes.tradeoffs: Additional maintenance on lineitem and less benefit for queries driven only by shipdate.

indexes.name: idx_lineitem_receipt_orderkey_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Motivated by Q11, Q20, and Q22 patterns that filter lineitem by receipt date ranges or receipt/commit conditions and then join or probe by order key and supplier key.
indexes.benefits: Helps receipt-date-driven scans while preserving useful trailing keys for order and supplier checks.
indexes.tradeoffs: Another index on the largest table increases insert/update cost and storage; overlaps partially with the l_orderkey index.

indexes.name: idx_orders_custkey_orderdate
indexes.workload_pattern: Supports customer-to-orders joins with date filtering in Q3, Q4, Q7, Q9, and anti-/semi-join probes in Q21. Existing index on o_orderdate alone does not cover the frequent join on o_custkey.
indexes.benefits: Improves access when starting from filtered customer rows and then restricting orders by date, and helps NOT EXISTS/LEFT JOIN patterns by customer.
indexes.tradeoffs: Moderate write/storage overhead on orders; some queries may still prefer the existing o_orderdate index.

indexes.name: idx_orders_orderstatus_orderkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Justified by Q20, which filters orders on o_orderstatus = 'F' and joins to lineitem by o_orderkey.
indexes.benefits: Allows quick retrieval of finished orders before joining to lineitem.
indexes.tradeoffs: Narrower applicability than other suggestions; modest maintenance overhead on orders.

indexes.name: idx_partsupp_partkey_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Supports repeated joins and correlated subqueries involving partsupp keyed by part and supplier in Q2, Q8, Q10, Q15, and Q19.
indexes.benefits: Improves joins from part to partsupp and efficient matching against supplier-specific rows.
indexes.tradeoffs: Extra index maintenance on partsupp; less useful for queries driven only by supplier.

indexes.name: idx_partsupp_suppkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Motivated by supplier-to-partsupp joins in Q2 and Q10 and by supplier filtering before aggregating/grouping partsupp rows.
indexes.benefits: Speeds lookup of all partsupp rows for selected suppliers and complements the part-led composite index.
indexes.tradeoffs: Adds another maintenance path on partsupp; benefit is smaller if plans consistently drive from part.

indexes.name: idx_customer_mktsegment_custkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Driven by Q3, which filters customer on c_mktsegment and immediately joins to orders on c_custkey.
indexes.benefits: Lets PostgreSQL isolate the requested market segment and feed matching customer keys into the orders join.
indexes.tradeoffs: Customer is relatively small, so gains are moderate.

indexes.name: idx_supplier_nationkey
indexes.workload_pattern: Used in Q2, Q4, Q8, Q10, Q19, and Q20 where supplier is joined to nation, often after region or nation-name filtering.
indexes.benefits: Helps join filtered nation rows to supplier and then onward into partsupp/lineitem.
indexes.tradeoffs: Supplier table is small, so benefit is modest.

global_observations: Existing single-column indexes on lineitem.l_shipdate and orders.o_orderdate already cover several date-range predicates, so additional shipdate-only and orderdate-only indexes were not duplicated.
The workload is TPC-H style: most benefit comes from indexing fact-table join keys and bridge-table composite keys rather than adding many low-value indexes on very small dimension tables.
Predicates with leading-wildcard LIKE or function-wrapped TEXT expressions are weak candidates for standard PostgreSQL B-tree recommendations under the allowed index types, so they were avoided.
Primary keys are not listed in metadata; recommendations focus on clearly missing join and filter paths without assuming hidden indexes.
